Notice Of Joinders Motion To Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Aug-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
KEITH E. EGGLETON, ESQ.(WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI), (650)320-4893, FOR DEFENDANTS/MOVING PARTIES, BIGBAND NETWORKS, INC., AMIR BASSAN-ESKENAZI, FREDERICK A. BALL, RAN OZ, LLOYD CARNEY, DEAN GILBERT, KENNETH GOLDMAN, GAL ISRAELY, BRUCE SACHS, ROBERT J. SACHS, GEOFFREY YANG
MICHAEL C. TU, ESQ.(ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP), (213)612-2433, FOR DEFENDANTS/MOVING PARTIES, MORGAN STANLEY & CO. INCORPORATED, MERRILL LYNCH, PIERCE, FENNER & SMITH INCORPORATED, JEFFERIES & COMPANY, INC., COWEN AND COMPANY, LLC, THINKPANMURE, LLC'S(FORMERLY KNOWN AS THINK EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC)
JEFFREY W. LAWRENCE, ESQ.(COUGHLIN STOIA GELLER RUDMAN & ROBBINS LLP), (415)288-4545, FOR PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY, JAMES WILTJER

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS MORGAN STANLEY & CO. INCORPORATED, MERRILL LYNCH, PIERCE, FENNER & SMITH INCORPORATED, JEFFERIES & COMPANY, INC., COWEN AND COMPANY, LLC, THINKPANMURE, LLC'S(FORMERLY KNOWN AS THINK EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC) Joinders Motion To Stay Proceedings IS GRANTED. IT IS NECESSARY AND APPROPRIATE TO STAY THIS ACTION IN FAVOR OF THE ESSENTIALLY IDENTICAL EARLIER-FILED CONSOLIDATED ACTIONS PENDING IN FEDERAL COURT IN THE NORTHERN DISTRICT OF CALIFORNIA IN ORDER TO AVOID CONFLICTING DECISIONS, AVOID WASTING JUDICIAL AND PARTY RESOURCES, AND AVOID AN UNSEEMLY RACE TO JUDGMENT AGAINST CLASS REPRESENTATIVES AND COUNSEL DULY CHOSEN BY THE FEDERAL COURT TO MANAGE THE LITIGATION OF CLASS MEMBERS' RIGHTS. THE STAY WILL ALLOW THE CLAIM TO GO FORWARD IN THE MORE APPROPRIATE FORUM AND WITHOUT SUBSTANTIALLY PREJUDICING PLAINTIFF'S LEGITIMATE INTERESTS. THE COURT GRANTED THE MOTION TO STAY WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO MOTION REQUESTING RELIEF FROM THE STAY. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 (301/PJB/AL)

BALL, RAN OZ, LLOYD CARNEY, DEAN GILBERT, KENNETH GOLDMAN, GAL ISRAELY, BRUCE SACHS, ROBERT J. SACHS, GEOFFREY YANG'S Motion To Stay Proceedings IS GRANTED. IT IS NECESSARY AND APPROPRIATE TO STAY THIS ACTION IN FAVOR OF THE ESSENTIALLY IDENTICAL EARLIER-FILED CONSOLIDATED ACTIONS PENDING IN FEDERAL COURT IN THE NORTHERN DISTRICT OF CALIFORNIA IN ORDER TO AVOID CONFLICTING DECISIONS, AVOID WASTING JUDICIAL AND PARTY RESOURCES, AND AVOID AN UNSEEMLY RACE TO JUDGMENT AGAINST CLASS REPRESENTATIVES AND COUNSEL DULY CHOSEN BY THE FEDERAL COURT TO MANAGE THE LITIGATION OF CLASS MEMBERS' RIGHTS. THE STAY WILL ALLOW THE CLAIM TO GO FORWARD IN THE MORE APPROPRIATE FORUM AND WITHOUT SUBSTANTIALLY PREJUDICING PLAINTIFF'S LEGITIMATE INTERESTS. THE COURT GRANTED THE MOTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO MOTION REQUESTING RELIEF FROM THE STAY. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 (301/PJB/AL)
